attention1.ptxt: When carrying out road tests, select a safe place without people and running vehicles to prevent any accident.

servinfosub.title: Seat
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that each front seat slides smoothly and locks securely at any position. Also check that reclining mechanism of each front seat allows the seatback to be locked at any angle.

servinfosub.title: Seat Belt
servinfosub.ptxt: Inspect seat belt systems including webbing, buckles, latch plates, retractors and anchors for damage or wear.
Check that each seat belt locks securely.

servinfosub.title: Battery Electrolyte Level Check
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that the electrolyte level of every battery cell is between the upper and lower level lines on the case. If battery is equipped with built-in indicator, check battery condition by the indicator.

servinfosub.title: Accelerator Pedal Operation
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that accelerator pedal operates smoothly without getting caught by or interfering any other part.

servinfosub.title: Engine Start

attention1.ptxt: Be sure to observe the following precautions to prevent personal injuries and property damages in the event of accidental movements of vehicle.

item.ptxt: Provide enough spaces around the vehicle.
Firmly apply both the parking brake and foot brakes.
Do not use the accelerator pedal.
Be ready to set ignition “OFF” when engine starts.

servinfosub.ptxt: On M/T vehicles, place the gearshift lever in “Neutral”, depress clutch pedal halfway and try to start. Starting motor should crank only when clutch pedal is fully depressed.
On CVT vehicles, try to start the engine in each select lever position. The starting motor should crank only in “P” (Park) or “N” (Neutral) range.

servinfosub.title: Exhaust System Check
servinfosub.ptxt: Check for leakage, cracks or loose supports.

item.ptxt: Clutch is completely released when clutch pedal is depressed.
No slipping clutch occurs when releasing pedal and accelerating.
Clutch itself is free from any abnormal conditions.

servinfosub.title: Gearshift or Select Lever
servinfosub.ptxt: Check gear shift or select lever for smooth movement to all positions and for good and correct response of transaxle in any position.
With CVT equipped vehicles, check that shift indicator indicates position correctly according to select lever position.
With CVT equipped vehicles, also check that vehicle is at complete stop when select lever is placed in “P” range and all brakes are released.

item.ptxt: Brakes do not generate abnormal noise.
Vehicle does not pull to one side when brakes are applied.

topic.title: Parking brake
topic.ptxt: Check that lever stroke is proper.

attention1.ptxt: Observe the following precautions when performing parking brake test on a slope.

item.ptxt: Make sure that no person and obstruction is in the way ahead.
Be prepared to apply foot brakes quickly in case of a vehicle movement.

topic.ptxt: Check that parking brake is fully effective when the vehicle is stopped on a slope by pulling parking brake lever all the way.

item.ptxt: Check that steering wheel is not instable and has no abnormally large resistance to turn.
Check that vehicle does not wander or pull to one side.
Check that speed changes responsively to accelerator positions.
Check that engine is free from abnormal noise and abnormal vibration.

servinfosub.title: Body, Wheel and Power Train Components
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that body, wheels and power train components are free from any abnormal conditions such as abnormal noise and vibration.

servinfosub.title: Meter and Gauge
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that speedometer, odometer, fuel gauge, etc. are operate accurately.

servinfosub.title: Light
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that all lights operate properly.

servinfosub.title: Windshield Defroster
servinfosub.ptxt: Periodically check that air comes out from defroster outlet when operating heater or air conditioning.
Set mode control lever to defroster position and fan switch lever to highest position for this check.
